content:
he area-average of orig_full's OKLab-L
    # downsampled to proxy dimensions — NOT the nearest corner of the full-res guide.
    g00 = tl.load(guide_proxy_ptr + py0c * pw + px0c, mask=mask)
    g01 = tl.load(guide_proxy_ptr + py0c * pw + px1c, mask=mask)
    g10 = tl.load(guide_proxy_ptr + py1c * pw + px0c, mask=mask)
    g11 = tl.load(guide_proxy_ptr + py1c * pw + px1c, mask=mask)

    # Spatial distances in proxy-pixel units
    dy0 = py_f - py0c.to(tl.float32)
    dy1 = py_f - py1c.to(tl.float32)
    dx0 = px_f - px0c.to(tl.float32)
    dx1 = px_f - px1c.to(tl.float32)

    # L channel weights (sigma_spatial_L)
    s2_L = sigma_spatial_L * sigma_spatial_L
    spatial00_L = tl.exp(-(dy0 * dy0 + dx0 * dx0) / (2.0 * s2_L))
    spatial01_L = tl.exp(-(dy0 * dy0 + dx1 * dx1) / (2.0 * s2_L))
    spatial10_L = tl.exp(-(dy1 * dy1 + dx0 * dx0) / (2.0 * s2_L))
    spatial11_L = tl.exp(-(dy1 * dy1 + dx1 * dx1) / (2.0 * s2_L))

    dg00 = g00 - g_out
    dg01 = g01 - g_out
    dg10 = g10 - g_out
    dg11 = g11 - g_out

    # Compute raw luma weights (no per-weight floor).
    # When the sum of all 4 luma weights collapses below 1e-4 the guide is
    # useless at this pixel (all neighbors lie on the wrong side of a sharp
    # edge).  In that case fall back to pure spatial bilinear (luma = 1 for
    # all 4), which is mathematically clean and avoids content-dependent
    # edge bleed that the old 1e-3 per-weight floor introduced.
    luma00 = tl.exp(-(dg00 * dg00) * sigma_luma_sq_inv * 0.5)
    luma01 = tl.exp(-(dg01 * dg01) * sigma_luma_sq_inv * 0.5)
    luma10 = tl.exp(-(dg10 * dg10) * sigma_luma_sq_inv * 0.5)
    luma11 = tl.exp(-(dg11 * dg11) * sigma_luma_sq_inv * 0.5)

    luma_sum = luma00 + luma01 + luma10 + luma11
